summary: PR 2265 updated lbarchive cleanup and bug fixes: renamed lbArchive_80017340 to lbArchiveRelocate across symbols, header, implementation, and ftdata call sites; fixed two varargs va_start calls in lbArchive_80017040 and lbArchive_800171CC to use the last named parameter before the ellipsis; removed lbArchive_LoadSections from lbarchive.c; and cleaned a shadowing redeclaration in lbArchive_vLoadSectionsFatal. The only loaded automated report shows a matched-code regression for lbArchive_LoadSections after its removal.

key_files.role: Primary implementation change: removed lbArchive_LoadSections, fixed va_start usage, cleaned symbol_name declaration, and renamed lbArchive_80017340 implementation to lbArchiveRelocate.
Header prototype updated from lbArchive_80017340 to lbArchiveRelocate.
Two call sites updated to use lbArchiveRelocate.
Symbol map renamed .text:0x80017340 from lbArchive_80017340 to lbArchiveRelocate.

what_changed.text: Renamed function symbol lbArchive_80017340 to lbArchiveRelocate at address .text:0x80017340 and propagated the rename through implementation, header, symbol map, and two ftdata call sites.

Changed va_start(args, filename) to va_start(args, symbols) in lbArchive_80017040 and lbArchive_800171CC, consistent with symbols being the last named parameter before varargs in the shown signatures.
Removed the lbArchive_LoadSections function body from lbarchive.c.
In lbArchive_vLoadSectionsFatal, changed an inner declaration from 'const char* symbol_name = va_arg(...)' to assignment to the already-declared symbol_name local.
The loaded bot report recorded matched code 55.87% (-0.01%, -240 bytes) and one broken match for main/melee/lb/lbarchive item lbArchive_LoadSections, changing from 100.00% before to 0.00% after.

smart_moves.text: The semantic rename was applied consistently across symbol metadata, public header, implementation, and known call sites in the diff rather than leaving a mixed address-style/semantic naming state.
The varargs fix targets the narrow va_start argument in the two affected functions without changing the broader loading logic shown in the diff.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When renaming an address-style function to a semantic name, keep symbol metadata, the owning header, implementation, and call sites synchronized in the same change so later reviewers do not see duplicate identities for one address.
For variadic functions, va_start should be checked against the actual last named parameter in the function signature; in this PR, symbols, not filename, is used in two lbarchive varargs wrappers.
Removing or renaming a matched function can create report noise even when the source cleanup is intentional; preserve the bot/report evidence rather than assuming the cleanup improved matching.

naming_conventions.name: lbArchiveRelocate
naming_conventions.meaning: Semantic replacement name for lbArchive_80017340 at .text:0x80017340.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: No assembly steering tactic is shown. The loaded evidence shows source-level cleanup and a bot report with a broken match for removed lbArchive_LoadSections.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Synchronized semantic function rename across source and symbols
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2265 demonstrates a semantic rename from lbArchive_80017340 to lbArchiveRelocate applied in config/GALE01/symbols.txt, src/melee/lb/lbarchive.c, src/melee/lb/lbarchive.h, and ftdata call sites. Candidate lesson: when evidence supports a semantic function name, update symbol metadata, owning header, implementation, and call sites together.

A possible standard note is to preserve report evidence and investigate intentional removals/renames that break matched-function accounting.
